evidence: We have invested over $76.7 billion in research and development since our inception, yielding inventions that are essential to modern computing.
More than half of our engineers work on software... 31,000 were engaged in research and development and 11,000 were engaged in sales, marketing, operations, and administrative positions.
NVIDIA is now a data center scale AI infrastructure company... In fiscal year 2026, we launched and scaled the NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra platform, optimized for agentic, reasoning, and physical AI.

NVIDIA NIM, which increases token throughput using industry-leading open and proprietary models; NVIDIA NeMo, a complete solution for curating, fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, evaluating, and safeguarding domain-adapted models.
Rubin platform... delivers up to a 10x reduction in cost per token compared to Blackwell.
NVIDIA powers over 78% of the supercomputers on the global TOP500 list, including 9 of the top 10 systems on the Green500 list.
Issues relating to the responsible use of our technologies, including AI, may result in reputational or financial harm and liability.

As of the end of fiscal year 2026, we were effectively foreclosed from competing in China's data center computing/compute market, and our effective foreclosure from the China market helped our competitors build larger developer and customer ecosystems.

The availability of data centers, energy, and capital to support the buildout of NVIDIA AI infrastructure by our customers and partners is crucial, and any shortage... could impact our future revenue and financial performance.

capex_mentions.quote: invested over $76.7 billion in research and development since our inception
capex_mentions.context: Cumulative R&D investment since founding, tied to AI and accelerated computing platform development

capex_mentions.quote: We entered into multi-year cloud service agreements to support our research and development activities
capex_mentions.context: Multi-year cloud service commitments for R&D, including AI development

capex_mentions.quote: We have also entered and may continue to enter into prepaid manufacturing and capacity agreements to supply both current and future products
capex_mentions.context: Prepaid manufacturing/capacity agreements to support AI data center product supply

limitations: The $76.7B R&D figure is cumulative since inception (1993), not a single-year or recent-period figure; annual AI-attributable R&D spend is not separately quantified in these excerpts.
Concreteness score of 5 is supported by quantified metrics (10x cost reduction, 78% TOP500 share) but some product claims (Rubin 10x) are forward-looking projections, not yet realized outcomes.

Risk score of 4 reflects company-specific mechanisms (China export foreclosure impacting revenue, energy/capital constraints on AI buildout) but no formal quantification of risk exposure or named AI risk governance body appears in these excerpts.
Excerpts are pre-filtered; additional capex or risk disclosures may exist in unexcerpted sections (e.g., MD&A, financial statements).
